title: Flight Level Assignment
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm separation minima, transition procedures, and local level allocation rules with current official sources.
learning_objectives: Assign a climb or descent to a target flight level.
Observe dynamic FL change from aircraft vertical performance.
Recognize when an aircraft has reached the assigned level.
key_points: The target FL is an instruction; the displayed FL changes over time.
Different aircraft types can climb or descend at different rates.
Do not assume an aircraft is separated vertically until the level change is established and sufficient.

lesson_steps.title: Target Versus Current FL
lesson_steps.body: The assigned FL is where the aircraft is cleared to go. The current FL is what the student must monitor while it climbs or descends.

lesson_steps.title: Vertical Trend
lesson_steps.body: Watch the label and selected-aircraft metadata after assigning a new FL. The value should change according to aircraft performance.

lesson_steps.title: Climb And Descend
script: Select another aircraft and assign descent to FL240.
Monitor both until they approach target levels.

exercise.title: Assign And Monitor Two Level Changes
exercise.objective: Issue one climb and one descent, then monitor the changing FL values.
student_actions: Select two aircraft on different routes.
Assign one climb and one descent.
Monitor current FL, target FL, and vertical rate.
success_conditions: Both aircraft receive valid target FL instructions.
The student observes dynamic FL change before assuming the level is reached.
debrief_prompts: Which aircraft reached its target faster?
How did the current FL differ from target FL during the climb or descent?
What would you check before using vertical separation?
